title: Motorcycle Classes & License Training Near Me
description: Find motorcycle classes and license training near you. Browse MSF-certified rider courses by state that teach skills and can waive your DMV road test.

children.value: Motorcycle Classes & License Training Near Me

Searching for a motorcycle class near you is the first step toward your license. An approved rider course teaches the skills you need, supplies the training motorcycle and helmet, and in most states waives the DMV road test when you finish. Below are MSF-certified and state-approved programs you can use to find training in your area.
Why Take a Local Motorcycle Class
Hands-on practice
on a provided motorcycle in a controlled range, not in traffic.
A road-test waiver
in most states, so you skip the DMV skills exam.
Insurance discounts
that many providers help you qualify for.
Confidence
built on the MSF Basic RiderCourse curriculum used nationwide.
Find a Course by State
California
1. CMSP - California Motorcyclists Safety Program
The statewide program offering the Motorcyclist Training Course and a DMV skills test waiver for all rider levels.
Learn more about CMSP
2. Pacific Safety Center
A San Diego provider delivering the CMSP Motorcyclist Training Course with provided motorcycles and helmets.
Learn more about Pacific Safety Center
Texas
3. San Antonio Motorcycle Training Center
MSF-affiliated center offering the Basic Rider Course and more, with sites across the greater Texas area.
Learn more about San Antonio Motorcycle Training Center
Oregon
4. Team Oregon
The statewide rider training organization, serving Oregon since 1984 with beginner and intermediate endorsement courses.
Learn more about Team Oregon
New York
5. Motorcycle Safety School
New York rider training offering the MSF Basic RiderCourse, BRC2 license waiver, and three-wheel courses.
Learn more about Motorcycle Safety School
Florida
6. SafeRide
Small-group Basic RiderCourse classes across six Florida locations, meeting state endorsement requirements.
Learn more about SafeRide
Maryland
7. Wor-Wic Community College
Certified weekend Basic Rider Courses with motorcycles provided, instructed by MVA- and MSF-certified coaches.
Learn more about Wor-Wic Community College
More Locations
Browsing from another state? Start with the
Motorcycle Safety Foundation
to find the national curriculum, or check our
requirements by state
hub for state-specific programs.
Frequently Asked Questions
How do I find a motorcycle class near me?
Pick your state above and follow the link to an approved provider, or
browse all listings
to filter by location and course type.
Do motorcycle classes include the license test?
In most states, completing an approved course earns a DMV skills test waiver, so the course effectively replaces the road test. See our
test waiver guide
Do I need my own motorcycle for the class?
Usually not. Most rider courses provide a training motorcycle and helmet for the riding portion. Confirm with the provider when you register.
How much does a motorcycle class cost?
Pricing varies by provider and state. Many beginner courses run a few hundred dollars and include the road-test waiver, which can offset DMV fees.
Ready to get licensed? Read the
step-by-step licensing guide
